review_notes: The functions provided are mostly accurate and align with the FedRAMP SC-7 (18) control for boundary protection and fail-secure configurations. The naming conventions are followed correctly, and the functions map well to Kubernetes security features. However, a minor improvement in the reasoning could be made to emphasize the importance of TLS in ingress resources for fail-secure configurations.
title: Boundary Protection | Fail Secure
step1_reasoning: The FedRAMP SC-7 (18) control focuses on ensuring boundary protection and fail-secure configurations, which in Kubernetes can be mapped to network security and ingress configurations. NetworkPolicies can enforce default deny rules for ingress and egress, ensuring that no unintended traffic flows in or out of the cluster. Additionally, ensuring that ingress resources use TLS and restricting the use of LoadBalancer services to prevent public exposure aligns with the fail-secure principle.
